content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Construct an array AAA of size NNN, with all non-zero elements, such that the sum of elements is 000 and that the absolute values of all elements do not exceed 333.

Formally, find an array AAA of NNN elements such that:


A1+A2+‚Ä¶+AN=0A_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_N = 0A1‚Äã+A2‚Äã+‚Ä¶+AN‚Äã=0
‚à£Ai‚à£|A_i|‚à£Ai‚Äã‚à£ is either 111, 222 or 333.
If multiple answers exist, all will be accepted. If no answer exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each input contains NNN - the size of the array.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2, \ldots A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶AN‚Äã satisfying the conditions or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if no solution.


If multiple answer exists, all will be accepted.

Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There exists no valid arrays of size 111.
Test Case 2 : The arrays [1,‚àí1][1, -1][1,‚àí1], [‚àí1,1][-1, 1][‚àí1,1], [2,‚àí2][2, -2][2,‚àí2], [‚àí2,2][-2, 2][‚àí2,2], [3,‚àí3][3, -3][3,‚àí3] and [‚àí3,3][-3, 3][‚àí3,3] are all accepted.
